docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Dagflow runs Dagster OSS entirely inside a consumer Snowflake account and security boundary using Snowpark Container Services. The Native App creates the Dagster control plane, code-location services, and autoscaling short-lived run-worker job services.

Dagflow integrates Dagster with Snowflake authentication, RBAC, image repositories, external access integrations, and secrets.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  provider["Provider<br/>Marketplace listing<br/>Native App package<br/>Container images"]

  subgraph consumer["Consumer Snowflake account"]
    users["Users and admins<br/>Snowflake identity + RBAC"]
    cli["Dagflow CLI<br/>snow dg"]

    subgraph app["Dagflow Native App"]
      subgraph control_pool["Control compute pool"]
        control["Dagflow control plane<br/>RBAC proxy + Dagster webserver<br/>Dagster daemon"]
        postgres["PostgreSQL service<br/>Dagster metadata"]
      end

      subgraph location_pool["Code-location compute pool"]
        locations["Code-location services<br/>PEX host + user definitions"]
      end

      subgraph run_pool["Run compute pool"]
        workers["Ephemeral run-worker<br/>job services"]
      end

      pex[("PEX stage<br/>source.pex + deps.pex")]
      logs[("Compute-log stage")]
    end

    resources["Consumer resources<br/>warehouses, databases,<br/>integrations, and secrets"]
  end

  provider -->|"Installs app and supplies images"| app
  users -->|"HTTPS service endpoint"| control
  cli -->|"Uploads artifacts"| pex
  cli -->|"Deploys code locations"| control
  control <-->|"Dagster metadata"| postgres
  control <-->|"gRPC"| locations
  pex -->|"Loads definitions"| locations
  control -->|"Launches each run"| workers
  locations -->|"Execution plan"| workers
  workers -->|"Run logs"| logs
  workers -->|"Granted access"| resources

The Native App and its services run inside the consumer account. Provider-owned images supply the runtime, while user code is deployed as PEX artifacts and runs with only the Snowflake resources granted by the consumer.

These docs are for:

  • Admins who install Dagflow, grant roles, add code locations, and operate the app.
  • Users who use the Dagster UI or APIs.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This website documents how to install, configure, and operate Dagflow. It does not replace the official Dagster documentation for Dagster APIs, definitions, assets, jobs, schedules, sensors, or general Dagster concepts.

For Dagster-specific usage, see the official Dagster documentation.

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Install or uninstall the Dagflow Native App.
  • Add or remove code locations.
  • Control app access with application roles.
  • Launch Dagster runs.
  • Update code locations.

docs.title: Where To Start
docs.text:
• Admins: Install Dagflow, then App API and Operations.
  • Users: RBAC, then Snowflake Run Tags.

docs.title: App API and Operations
docs.text: App management procedures live in <app_name>.app_public. APP_ADMIN can call all procedures on this page. APP_EDITOR can deploy and remove code locations.

docs.title: start_app
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    start_app()
    Creates the Dagflow compute pools and starts or updates the core.postgres and core.dagster services.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.start_app();

docs.title: grant_callback
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    grant_callback(privileges ARRAY)
    Retries the same startup callback that Snowflake invokes when required app privileges are granted. Use this only when startup did not happen automatically after granting CREATE COMPUTE POOL and BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description privileges Yes Array of granted privilege names. The app starts only when the array contains both CREATE COMPUTE POOL and BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.grant_callback([
  'CREATE COMPUTE POOL',
  'BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT'
]);

docs.title: service_status
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    service_status()
    Shows services in the app core schema.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_status();

docs.title: dagster_containers
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    dagster_containers()
    Shows containers for the core.dagster service.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.dagster_containers();

docs.title: dagster_logs
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    dagster_logs(
  container_name STRING,
  line_count NUMBER
)
    Returns logs from a core.dagster container.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description container_name Yes Dagster service container name. Use webserver or daemon. line_count No Number of log lines to return. Defaults to 100 when NULL.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.dagster_logs('webserver', 200);
CALL dagflow_app.app_public.dagster_logs('daemon', NULL);

docs.title: Code Locations
docs.text:
Code-location management is handled by the Dagflow server API, not Native App stored procedures. Prefer the CLI:

snow dg code-location add finance \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --version abc123 \
  --module-name finance.definitions \
  --source-pex dist/source.pex \
  --deps-pex dist/deps.pex \
  --blocking

Low-level clients can upload PEX artifacts to @core.pex/<location>/<version>/ and call:

  • PUT /code-locations/{name} with JSON body {"version":"abc123","entrypoint":{"type":"module","name":"finance.definitions"},"blocking":true}.
  • DELETE /code-locations/{name} to remove a location.

Required role: APP_ADMIN or APP_EDITOR.

docs.title: restart_dagster
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    restart_dagster()
    Suspends and resumes the core.dagster service. Use this when the control plane needs a manual restart.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.restart_dagster();

docs.title: suspend_services
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    suspend_services()
    Suspends the core.dagster and core.postgres services. Compute pools are left running.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.suspend_services();

docs.title: suspend_compute_pools
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    suspend_compute_pools()
    Suspends the app control, code-location, and run compute pools. Services are not explicitly suspended first.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.suspend_compute_pools();

docs.title: suspend_app
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    suspend_app()
    Suspends core.dagster, core.postgres, and all Dagflow compute pools.

  • Arguments: none.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.suspend_app();

docs.title: service_containers
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    service_containers(service_name STRING)
    Shows containers for a service in the app core schema.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description service_name Yes Core service name without schema, for example dagster or postgres. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_containers('dagster');

docs.title: service_logs
docs.text:
• Signature: 

    service_logs(
  service_name STRING,
  container_name STRING,
  line_count NUMBER
)
    Returns logs from a service container in the app core schema.

  • Arguments:

Argument Required Description service_name Yes Core service name without schema, for example dagster or postgres. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores. container_name Yes Container name. Must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens. line_count No Number of log lines to return. Defaults to 100 when NULL.

  • Required role: APP_ADMIN.

  • Example: 

    CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_logs('dagster', 'webserver', 500);
CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_logs('postgres', 'postgres', NULL);
    Use these procedures when the UI is unavailable, a code update is missing, or Snowflake reports an unhealthy service.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
You need a Snowflake role that can install the Dagflow Native App and grant its requested privileges. The app needs account-level privileges to create compute pools and bind service endpoints.

You also need PEX artifacts for each Dagster code location: source.pex for project code and deps.pex for the complete runtime, including direct requirements on dagster and dagster-postgres.

Both runtime requirements must resolve from the package index used by the project build. dagster-postgres must be the companion release compatible with the project's Dagster version; run workers use its storage classes when they reconstruct the control-plane instance. The provider-owned runtime image supplies Dagflow's compute-log manager without installing its dependencies.

docs.title: Install the App
docs.text:
If you install from the Snowflake Marketplace UI, follow the UI prompts and skip this section. To run the same steps manually, continue below.

After installation, grant requested privileges if they were not auto-granted:

GRANT CREATE COMPUTE POOL ON ACCOUNT TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT BIND SERVICE ENDPOINT ON ACCOUNT TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

Snowflake invokes the app grant callback after required privileges are granted. To retry startup manually:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.start_app();
CALL dagflow_app.app_public.service_status();

Grant Dagflow application roles to users or roles that need the Dagster UI:

GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_VIEWER TO ROLE <viewer_role>;
GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_LAUNCHER TO ROLE <launcher_role>;
GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_EDITOR TO ROLE <editor_role>;
GRANT APPLICATION ROLE dagflow_app.APP_ADMIN TO ROLE <admin_role>;

Use the least powerful role that fits each user. See RBAC for allowed actions.

docs.title: Prepare PEX Artifacts
docs.text: Build your Dagster project into source.pex and deps.pex, then upload those files to the app-managed PEX stage with AUTO_COMPRESS=FALSE. Code-location and run-worker images contain only the Dagster-free bootstrap; they never provide a fallback Dagster installation.

docs.title: Add a Code Location
docs.text:
Upload and deploy the code-location version:

snow dg code-location add finance \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --version 2026-05-29 \
  --module-name finance.definitions \
  --source-pex source.pex \
  --deps-pex deps.pex \
  --blocking

Dagflow creates an app-owned code location named FINANCE with the provider-owned runtime image and records the desired PEX version in the service comment.

Pass --no-blocking when automation should return after requesting the service update instead of waiting for readiness.

After the service is ready, open the Dagster UI from the Snowflake service endpoint for core.dagster.

docs.title: Compute Resources
docs.text:
Dagflow creates three SPCS compute pools in the consumer account:

Compute pool Used for Instance family Nodes <app_name>_CONTROL_POOL Dagster UI, daemon, and storage GEN_ARM_G1_8 1 <app_name>_CODE_LOCATION_POOL Registered code locations GEN_ARM_G1_4 1-20 <app_name>_RUN_POOL Pipeline run workers GEN_ARM_G1_8 1-50

Run workers default to 0.25 vCPU and 512Mi memory per run. Users can request different resources with snowflake/cpu and snowflake/memory tags.

docs.title: RBAC
docs.text: Dagflow uses Snowflake application roles to control Dagster operations. Admins grant these roles to Snowflake account roles; users do not manage them directly.

docs.title: Roles
Choosing Roles
docs.text:
• Give APP_VIEWER to users who inspect jobs, assets, run history, and logs.
  • Give APP_LAUNCHER to users who launch or stop runs.
  • Give APP_EDITOR to users who manage schedules, sensors, backfills, concurrency, or PEX code-location versions and reloads.
  • Give APP_ADMIN only to Dagflow operators.

docs.title: Operation Mapping
docs.text:
Roles are hierarchical. APP_EDITOR includes APP_LAUNCHER; APP_ADMIN includes all permissions below.

Operation Required role launchRun APP_LAUNCHER launchPipelineExecution APP_LAUNCHER launchRunReexecution APP_LAUNCHER launchPipelineReexecution APP_LAUNCHER terminateRun APP_LAUNCHER terminatePipelineExecution APP_LAUNCHER terminateRuns APP_LAUNCHER deleteRun APP_LAUNCHER deletePipelineRun APP_LAUNCHER startSchedule APP_EDITOR stopRunningSchedule APP_EDITOR resetSchedule APP_EDITOR scheduleDryRun APP_EDITOR startSensor APP_EDITOR stopSensor APP_EDITOR resetSensor APP_EDITOR setSensorCursor APP_EDITOR sensorDryRun APP_EDITOR wipeAssets APP_EDITOR reportRunlessAssetEvents APP_EDITOR setAutoMaterializePaused APP_EDITOR launchPartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR launchBackfill APP_EDITOR cancelPartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR resumePartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR reexecutePartitionBackfill APP_EDITOR addDynamicPartition APP_EDITOR deleteDynamicPartitions APP_EDITOR launchMultipleRuns APP_EDITOR setConcurrencyLimit APP_EDITOR deleteConcurrencyLimit APP_EDITOR freeConcurrencySlotsForRun APP_EDITOR freeConcurrencySlots APP_EDITOR reloadRepositoryLocation APP_EDITOR reloadWorkspace APP_ADMIN shutdownRepositoryLocation APP_ADMIN

docs.title: Snowflake Run Tags
docs.text: This page is for users who launch runs and admins who grant Snowflake access. Set these Dagster run tags only when a run needs custom worker resources, external access integrations, or Snowflake secrets.

docs.title: CPU and Memory
docs.text:
Use snowflake/cpu and snowflake/memory to request CPU and memory for one run.

By default, run workers use 0.25 vCPU and 512Mi memory. This matches Dagster ECS' default Linux run-task resources. If dagster.yaml sets launcher-level run_resources, these tags override them for the run.

Example:

{
  "snowflake/cpu": "0.5",
  "snowflake/memory": "2Gi"
}

CPU values must be positive vCPU quantities such as 0.5, 1, or 500m. Memory values must include a Snowflake-supported unit: M, Mi, G, or Gi.

docs.title: External Access Integrations
docs.text:
Use snowflake/external_access_integrations when a run needs outbound network access allowed by a Snowflake external access integration.

The value can be a comma-separated string:

OPENAI_EAI,SLACK_EAI

Or a JSON string array:

["OPENAI_EAI", "SLACK_EAI"]

Each value must be an unquoted Snowflake identifier.

The installed application must have USAGE on every integration used by the run:

GRANT USAGE ON INTEGRATION OPENAI_EAI TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
Use snowflake/secrets when a run needs Snowflake secrets as environment variables. The value must be a JSON string array:

[
  {
    "snowflakeSecret": "DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE.API_TOKEN",
    "secretKeyRef": "secret_string",
    "envVarName": "MY_API_KEY"
  }
]

Fields:

  • snowflakeSecret: Snowflake secret name, unqualified or dotted, such as DATABASE.SCHEMA.SECRET_NAME.
  • secretKeyRef: key exposed by the Snowflake secret, such as secret_string or password.
  • envVarName: environment variable name inside the run worker.

The installed application needs USAGE on the secret schema and READ on each secret:

GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;
GRANT READ ON SECRET DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE.API_TOKEN
  TO APPLICATION dagflow_app;

Secret injection is independent from external access integrations. If a run sets both tags, Snowflake validates whether the integration allows the selected secrets.

docs.title: Example Dagster Launch Tags
docs.text:
When launching a run through Dagster GraphQL or any Dagster API that accepts execution metadata, pass tags as strings:

[
  {
    "key": "snowflake/cpu",
    "value": "0.5"
  },
  {
    "key": "snowflake/memory",
    "value": "2Gi"
  },
  {
    "key": "snowflake/external_access_integrations",
    "value": "OPENAI_EAI"
  },
  {
    "key": "snowflake/secrets",
    "value": "[{\"snowflakeSecret\":\"DAGFLOW_CONSUMER_DB.EAI_PROBE.API_TOKEN\",\"secretKeyRef\":\"secret_string\",\"envVarName\":\"MY_API_KEY\"}]"
  }
]

Dagflow validates tag values before creating the run-worker job service. Invalid resources, identifiers, JSON, duplicate environment variable names, or missing required fields fail the launch.

docs.title: Code Location
docs.text:
Dagster separates the control plane from user code. See the Dagster code locations architecture for details.

To update a code location, build a new source.pex and deps.pex, upload them to the app-managed PEX stage, and point the service comment at the new version with deploy_code_location.

docs.title: Build PEX Artifacts
docs.text:
The runtime expects two files per code-location version:

  • source.pex: your Dagster definitions and project code.
  • deps.pex: the project's runtime distributions, including Dagster and its PostgreSQL storage integration. The runtime image supplies Dagflow's compute-log manager without dependencies.

Example project layout:

.
|-- requirements.txt
`-- finance
    |-- __init__.py
    `-- definitions.py

Example requirements.txt:

dagster==1.12.1
dagster-postgres==0.28.1
snowflake-connector-python>=3.15,<4

dagster and dagster-postgres must be direct project requirements. This makes the selected deps.pex the only Dagster environment used by both the code server and its run workers. The provider-owned image supplies Dagflow's runtime extension without dependencies. Use the dagster-postgres companion release compatible with the selected Dagster version. Optional and development dependency groups are not bundled automatically.

The builder combines base dependencies declared in requirements.txt, setup.py, and pyproject.toml. Relative local packages are built into the source artifact recursively. Virtual environments, VCS metadata, caches, and build output are excluded from the filtered working source.

Use a stable version string when staging PEX artifacts:

@dagflow_app.core.pex/finance/2026-06-01/source.pex
@dagflow_app.core.pex/finance/2026-06-01/deps.pex

docs.title: Deploy with Snowflake CLI
docs.text:
Install the Dagflow CLI plugin in the same environment as Snowflake CLI:

pip install dagflow-cli-plugin

Enable the external plugin in your Snowflake CLI config:

[cli.plugins.dg]
enabled = true

Then use your existing Snowflake CLI connection to build, upload, and deploy. For a Dagster dg project, Dagflow infers the code-location name and target module from [tool.dg]:

snow dg deploy \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --path . \
  --version 2026-06-01 \
  --blocking

For a non-dg project, pass the code-location name and one explicit entrypoint:

snow dg deploy finance \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --path . \
  --version 2026-06-01 \
  --module-name finance.definitions \
  --blocking

The plugin builds source.pex and deps.pex in a temporary directory, uploads them to @dagflow_app.core.pex/finance/2026-06-01/, and calls the Dagflow server code-location API. Use code-location package when you want to keep the generated files locally for inspection or reuse. Use --python-version 3.12 when deploying to the Python 3.12 runtime. If the requested version differs from the active CLI Python, a matching interpreter such as python3.12 must be on PATH.

If you have already built PEX artifacts, use code-location add to upload and register them without packaging the project again:

snow dg code-location add finance \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --version 2026-06-01 \
  --module-name finance.definitions \
  --source-pex dist/dagflow/finance/2026-06-01/source.pex \
  --deps-pex dist/dagflow/finance/2026-06-01/deps.pex \
  --blocking

Launch a job with Dagster-style job launch naming:

snow dg job launch \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --location finance \
  --job my_job \
  --config run-config.yaml \
  --tag snowflake/cpu=500m

You can also build artifacts without deploying:

snow dg code-location package . \
  --location-name finance \
  --version 2026-06-01 \
  --module-name finance.definitions

code-location package writes local artifacts under dist/dagflow/<location>/<version>/ unless you pass --output-dir.

docs.title: Update an Existing Code Location
docs.text:
An APP_EDITOR can update code by deploying a new PEX version:

snow dg code-location add finance \
  --app-name dagflow_app \
  --version 2026-06-01 \
  --module-name finance.definitions \
  --source-pex dist/dagflow/finance/2026-06-01/source.pex \
  --deps-pex dist/dagflow/finance/2026-06-01/deps.pex \
  --blocking

Use --no-blocking when a release script should return after requesting the service update instead of waiting for the new code server to become ready.

docs.title: Refresh Dagster
docs.text:
Updating an existing code location does not require reinstalling Dagflow. The Dagflow server API updates the service comment, then reloads the code location in Dagster. That calls the PEX host's ReloadCode gRPC method, which activates the latest requested version before Dagster refreshes metadata.

Adding or removing a code location changes the Snowflake service list. The webserver and daemon refresh their workspace from running services in the code-location schema, so code-location API calls do not restart core.dagster.

Reload one deployed location from the CLI:

snow dg code-location reload finance --app-name dagflow_app

Reload the full Dagster workspace after out-of-band service changes:

snow dg code-location reload-all --app-name dagflow_app

Restart the control plane manually when needed:

CALL dagflow_app.app_public.restart_dagster();

docs.title: Remove a Code Location
docs.text:
Remove code locations that should no longer appear in Dagster:

snow dg code-location delete finance --app-name dagflow_app

Dagflow drops the code-location service through the server API. The webserver and daemon stop listing it after workspace refresh.
